name: Thai Grilled Chicken- Midsummer Thai Dinner
recipeIngredient: 1/2 teaspoon Chinese chili sauce or chili flakes
recipeInstructions:
If you prefer not to use coriander, substitute basil or mint instead. For a spicier dish, increase the chili sauce.
In a blender or by hand, combine coriander, chili sauce, soy sauce, oyster sauce, garlic and sugar. Marinate chicken for 4 hours or overnight.
Preheat broiler or barbecue. Broil chicken skin side down for 4 minutes. Turn and broil another 4 minutes until the skin is crispy and the chicken is cooked through. Cut into half-inch strips.
Place lettuce leaves on a platter and arrange chicken on top. Garnish with coriander, mint sprigs and green onions.
To eat in the Thai style, roll chicken in lettuce leaves along with herbs and onions. Dip into dipping sauce. Alternatively, serve lettuce, chicken, and garnishes together.
Tart Sweet Dipping Sauce
This is the traditional Thai dipping sauce used for salad rolls, spring rolls, and most everything else.
Combine sugar, water, and vinegar in a pot. Bring to a boil and continue boiling for 5 minutes. Stir in garlic, fish sauce, and chili sauce. Simmer for 2 minutes. Add lime juice and shredded carrot, then simmer for another minute.
